focus: Q3 - Define MiniMax 2.7 prompt-quality/RCAF guidance and safe --variant mapping policy

graphEvents.label: MiniMax uses existing RCAF/CLEAR fast path
MiniMax direct provider omits --variant by default until proven
MiniMax prompt quality reuses sk-prompt RCAF guidance
Unverified MiniMax variant mapping constrains default to unset
Ablation can promote variant mapping only with docs or observable provider option change
Highspeed should be selected as model id, not inferred from --variant high

executor.notes: manual deep-research iteration; no sub-agents dispatched

label: MiniMax should reuse the existing RCAF/CLEAR fast path rather than introduce a new prompt framework
Direct MiniMax dispatch should omit --variant by default until provider docs or live ablation prove a mapping
MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed should be routed as a distinct model id if exposed, not inferred from --variant high
MiniMax prompt quality guidance should emphasize visible task state, concise constraint rationale, exact file/model anchors, and active tool-round continuity
sk-prompt-models should add only link-only pointers to cli-opencode-owned MiniMax prompt-quality and variant-ablation policy
Do not assert that --variant high changes MiniMax reasoning without official docs or live ablation evidence
Do not duplicate prompt-quality bodies inside sk-prompt-models; it remains a thin sentinel and pattern index
OpenCode docs support provider/model variants generally but do not prove a MiniMax-specific built-in variant mapping
MiniMax docs expose MiniMax-M2.7 and MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed as separate model IDs with the same context window and different speed profiles
Local cli_reference.md already marks MiniMax --variant behavior as unverified in the phase-001 matrix row

reason: MiniMax documents highspeed as a separate model id/pricing-speed class, and OpenCode/MiniMax docs do not prove that --variant high selects it
Existing RCAF/CRISPE/TIDD-EC/CLEAR guidance already covers the task shapes; MiniMax needs context hygiene notes, not a framework fork
